programme.desc: In the Quickfire, it’s a true test of communication as the chefs play a game of telephone with their loved ones as they try to reproduce the dish their family member is currently eating at a nearby restaurant. Then, the chefs are tasked with creating a signature product that they will bottle, use on a dish and sell at a food festival. They may get help from their visiting loved ones, but for one chef, the help won’t save them from being sent home. Celebrity Chef Nancy Silverton is the guest judge joining Tom, Padma and Gail.

programme.title: The Epic Tales of Captain Underpants
programme.desc: In the season opener, George and Harold have returned to school due to a technicality, but the school has been taken over by Melvin and a cyborg Melvin from the future! Their goal is to make the school extremely difficult by replacing all the teachers with Teachertron robots. Suffering under the new regime and without Mr. Krupp/Captain Underpants on hand to help, the boys hypnotize gym teacher Mr. Meaner and turn him into Sergeant Boxers to fight back.

programme.title: The Epic Tales of Captain Underpants
programme.desc: Tired of the Melvins ruling the school, the boys realize that they need Mr. Krupp to make the school at least bearable. Unfortunately, Krupp has run off to Ecuador to look for a fabled fountain. So the boys form the M.I.S.F.A.R.T.S. - Mega Incredible Search Force Adventure Rescue Team Squad - to travel to Ecuador and bring Krupp back, whether he wants it or not.

programme.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge
programme.desc: Hiccup, Mala, Astrid, Fishlegs, and Snotlout go searching for the now captured Eruptodon, but little do they know that Viggo has a sinister plan.

programme.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge
programme.desc: When a distant cousin of the Twins named Gruffnut arrives on Dragon's Edge, Tuffnut tries to impress him, but Gruffnut may not be the hero he seems.

programme.title: Dragons: Race to the Edge
programme.desc: When Viggo puts a bounty on Hiccup's head, every Dragon Hunter in the world is after capturing Hiccup, so Toothless and the Riders must rescue him.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When Eep has the first-ever sleepover, scary noises and shadows send the girls screaming out of the cave; Thunk saves the day when Bear Owls invade.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When Grug takes Ugga hunting to find her inner growl again, she's as fierce as ever until coming face-to-face with a mom and baby too cute to catch.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When The Croods experience the first-ever sugar-rush, they're hooked on the delicious new food; Grug is labeled a zombie after taking the first nap.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When Grug won't let her join his winning team, Eep forms her own underdog team in the first-ever sports competition – Ahhh! Valley's annual Egg Toss.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When Thunk is so stinky he kills animals with his stench, he pretends to be a great hunter; A spider ant bite gives Grug the biggest brain in town.

programme.title: Dawn of the Croods
programme.desc: When Gran's cane and Sandy's bear go missing in the middle of the night, Eep chases down every clue to the first-ever mystery to find the thief.

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: During a camping trip Jim gives Lucky and the girls a treasure map that sends them on the adventure of a lifetime

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: Lucky Spirit and Turo team up to help save an abused horse

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: When a sudden blizzard hits Miradero, Lucky and Maricela must work together to save the town.

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: Grayson wrangles Spirit "fair and square" and sends him to auction in another town --and there is nothing Lucky can do to stop him!

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: Spirit and Lucky desperately try to find a way back to one another.

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free
programme.desc: The girl’s plans to attend the Governor’s Ball are waylaid when they save a pregnant horse.

programme.title: Turbo F.A.S.T.
programme.desc: Skid unleashes an evil robot on Turbotown and has to get rid of the monster himself; Turbo falls for and betroths a head-eating mantis princess.

programme.title: Turbo F.A.S.T.
programme.desc: White Shadow is mistaken for a celebrity snail and kidnapped; Tito gets a foosball table which quickly becomes a soccer battle ground.

programme.title: Archibald's Next Big Thing is Here!
programme.desc: Archibald and Sage enter an endurance race through the corn maze to end all corn mazes. / Thinking his new sneakers have made him super-fast, Archibald gets roped into delivering ice cream for a couple of new-to-town entrepreneurs.

programme.title: Archibald's Next Big Thing is Here!
programme.desc: In an effort to make sure he stays safe while home alone, Loy leaves Archibald with supervising robots. But Archibald turns them into robotic versions of his siblings. / While on a trip with his siblings, Archibald injures his legs skiing and is forced to stay in the lodge. But when he spots the rumored Phantom of Pointy Peak, Archibald gets wrapped up in the mystery.

programme.title: Spirit Riding Free: Riding Academy
programme.desc: With academy life on the horizon, the PALs work together to tame wild yearling Governor before they leave. But maybe he's not meant to be broken.

programme.title: Voltron: Legendary Defender
programme.desc: Coran reveals the origin story of Voltron and the original paladins

programme.title: Team Zenko Go
programme.desc: When Mr. Tanaka adopts an unruly dog at Sam’s behest, the Zenkos have the job of training the mischievous mutt and turning him into man’s best friend material. / When Ellie's friend Maria is late submitting her painting to the art show, it’s up to the Zenkos to get inside the community center after hours, avoiding overzealous junior security Rodney, and get Maria’s painting on display before the show starts.

programme.title: Veggietales: In the House
programme.desc: LarryBoy's friends plan a surprise party but he thinks they're up to no good/Madame Blueberry becomes the best player on Bob and Larry's soccer team

programme.title: Veggietales: In the House
programme.desc: Larry gets a chef's hat and convinces everyone that he's an expert chef, but it's really Bob who's doing all the cooking/Bob, Larry, and Mayor Archibald get lost while hiking in the backyard.

programme.title: Not Quite Narwhal
programme.desc: Pixie loves to ride the clouds, but she can't understand why Kelp doesn’t love it, too. If they don't agree on this, does that mean they're not best friends anymore? / Kelp helps Ollie fulfill his dream of watching the white whale migration.

programme.title: Not Quite Narwhal
programme.desc: When shark and pilot fish are separated it’s up to Kelp, Scallop, Cruz, and Waverly to help them find each other again. / Scallop’s friend Theo finally comes clean on why he’s always rushing off.

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse
programme.desc: It's Christmas time in the dollhouse! After putting the final topper on the Christmas tree, Figgy the Elf calls and says that Santa Kitty needs help in his workshop, it's up to Gabby and Pandy to go help her out. After some toy fixing and boxing fun, Gabby and Pandy get to meet Santa Kitty and his Kitty reindeer and join him delivering presents to all their friends in the dollhouse.

programme.title: Gabby's Dollhouse
programme.desc: The dollhouse delivery is a spaceship that takes off with a sleeping CatRat inside! Gabby, Pandy, and Kitty Fairy have to get the ship back into The Fairy Tail Garden before Floyd (who thinks it's the best kitty toy ever!) gets his paws on it!

programme.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders
programme.desc: Magnus's nephew, Axel, arrives on the island and tells the Rescue Riders that he wants to join their team. But is he just a trickster like his uncle?

programme.title: Dragons: Rescue Riders

programme.title: Team Zenko Go
programme.desc: After Lula loses her loose tooth in the park, Team Zenko must find the missing molar and get it under Lula’s pillow in time for the Tooth Fairy’s visit. / Gabriel and Luis show off their new drone but it crash lands on the roof top. Team Zenko's mission is to try and get it back, but Yolanda won't give up her new hi-tech friend that easily.

programme.title: Dew Drops Diaries
programme.desc: Marcus has a piano recital and unknowingly forgets his lucky penny at home. programme.title: Go, Dog. Go!
programme.desc: Tag and Scooch play Scooch’s favorite board game outside, using the whole town of Pawston as the board. \Tag thinks it’s time for an update to the Pawston City Anthem, which inspires a city-wide music competition where competitors will create and perform new Pawston anthems.

programme.title: Go, Dog. Go!
programme.desc: When Tag is left in charge of the Pawston Pawtomotive for the day, Frank and Beans open up a rival garage and take all the business. But when all the cars around Pawston break down because of Frank's shoddy mechanic work, Tag and Scooch have to go around town to fix all his mistakes. \ Scooch wants to be a Tail-kwondo master like his hero, Master Wag, but he can't break a biscuit with his tail. Tag helps train him (and his tail), and they even track down Master Wag who gives Scooch a good luck charm. But when Scooch loses the good luck charm before his tail-kwondo test, he needs to find a way to believe in himself.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That? S2
programme.desc: Host Mike Davidson goes to the remote Atacama desert in Chile and to one of the most incredible and complex observatories on Earth - ALMA. He works alongside ALMA's expert team as they repair some of the world's biggest antennas.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That? S2
programme.desc: The worldwide pandemic ceased operations on the world's only underwater habitat. Mike Davidson joins a crack repair crew as they dive in to bring it back to life and get NASA astronauts back inside for training.

programme.title: How Did They Fix That? S2
programme.desc: Mike Davidson joins the US Marines' 'Heavy Haulers' squadron as they're put to the test. They battle to keep their fleet of CH53 'Super Stallion' choppers flying, in preparation for intense warfare training exercises in the desert and mountains.

programme.title: Air Warriors S11
programme.desc: This documentary series shows five generations of jet engines. These fighter jets are capable of carrying bombs and soar close to the edge of space. The sixth generation of fighter jets began in 2020, and the 11th season has covered most of the air artillery since then.

programme.title: Wild Heroes
programme.desc: We follow Auckland Zoo’s bird team into the wilds of the Coromandel Ranges as they help to save native kiwi populations, It’s a feeding frenzy for the Tasmanian devils and the elephant team faces their biggest challenge yet.

programme.title: Wild Heroes
programme.desc: This time on Wild Heroes, there's shocking news for the elephant team, the vets are concerned about one of their rescued Little penguins, and we find out how it really does take a community to feed the Zoo.

programme.title: Wild Heroes
programme.desc: This time on Wild Heroes, Little penguin Snapper returns to the colony but will he be accepted? The vet team prepare to treat Samantran tiger Sali, and the Zoo helps to save an endangered native species at risk.

programme.title: Wild Heroes

programme.title: Wild Heroes
programme.desc: Baby Giraffe meets the herd, the Zoo’s five otter brothers are on the move and the ectotherm team search for a hidden for a treasure.

programme.title: Wild Heroes
programme.desc: This time on Wild Heroes, it's an anxious time for Zoo vets as they work to save one of the most endangered birds on the planet, and the ectotherm team are on a mission in the wild South Island.

programme.title: Loch Lomond: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: It is now June, and the park is bathed in 17 hours of daylight. With the warm temperatures come rich pickings, but for the newborn the summer months are crucial as they learn to fight, find food and avoid predators.

programme.title: Loch Lomond: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Autumn is when stags compete to breed, osprey chicks begin a 3000-mile migration to West Africa, salmon return to the rivers where they were born and adders give birth to live young.

programme.title: Loch Lomond: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: The winter is mild and wet, which is good news for some but bad news for others. Red deer are forced to higher ground, salmon have gone six months without food, mountain hares turn a conspicuous white, and squirrels kick their own young out of the nest.

programme.title: Iceland: The Puffin Sanctuary
programme.desc: The endearing, "comical" puffin is Iceland's national emblem, but its fate is concerning. For a decade, couples have stopped breeding. A handful of local enthusiasts are now mobilized to save this beloved seabird.

programme.title: In the Giant Mammoth Tracks
programme.desc: The million-year-old, giant Durfort mammoth skeleton required urgent, world-first restoration. We follow the tricky process of disassembling and reassembling the fragile fossil. Through CGI and museum knowledge, the film brings this fascinating, little-known giant back to life.

programme.title: Revisited
programme.desc: Revisited presents certain places in different parts of the world several years after some significant events or accidents. See what the current situation is and find out what people think and feel after a while. Are there any changes?

programme.title: The World this Week - Episode 16
programme.desc: Girish Sawlani brings you a weekly roundup of international news from an Australian perspective as reported by ABC News correspondents covering the Indo Pacific, Europe, North America, the Middle East, India and Indonesia.

programme.title: Compass - Surviving Gaza, Seeking Truth
programme.desc: Filmmaker Patrick Abboud meets frontline Palestinian journalists who risked everything to document the truth of Gaza. Now safe in Australia, they reveal extraordinary new evidence of the continued killing of journalists.

programme.title: Project Wild - Tonga
programme.desc: Nature nerd, Dr Ann Jones travels through the Indo-Pacific region to find the dedicated people bringing our planet's amazing animals, plants and ecosystems back from the brink. This time Ann explores the Kingdom of Tonga.

programme.title: I was actually there - Port Arthur Massacre
programme.desc: On an autumn morning at Port Arthur's historic convict settlement, a gunman walked into the Broad Arrow Cafe, killing 35 people. We go deep inside the experience, hear from first hand witnesses to understand what happened.

programme.title: Dr Karl's How Things Work - Ice Cream
programme.desc: Dr Karl gets the scoop on how two of our most popular ice creams are made. Australians each devour around 18 litres of ice cream a year, making us the third largest population of ice cream eaters on the planet.

programme.title: AFL Premiership Season - Round 9 - Richmond v Adelaide Crows
programme.desc: ABC Australia brings you Australian Rules football live in your lounge room every week with coverage of the 2026 AFL Premiership Season.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero - Episode 6
programme.desc: Join acclaimed Torres Strait Islander chef and cultural ambassador Nornie Bero in Island Echoes, a vibrant journey through the breathtaking landscapes and rich traditions of her homeland.

programme.title: Field to Feast - Episode 2
programme.desc: You've heard of green asparagus but have you ever tried white asparagus. Thanh heads to a local farm to learn more about this fascinating variety. Topped off with a fine dining experience at a hip Melbourne restaurant.

programme.title: ABC National News - Episode 130
programme.desc: There's never been a more important time for transparency and trust. ABC News brings you today's top stories and latest news, with more reporters to tell you what's going on and why it matters. Presented by Meredith Sheehan.

programme.title: Compass - Surviving Gaza, Seeking Truth
programme.desc: Filmmaker Patrick Abboud meets frontline Palestinian journalists who risked everything to document the truth of Gaza. Now safe in Australia, they reveal extraordinary new evidence of the continued killing of journalists.

programme.title: Off Country - Episode 2
programme.desc: Xyz, Chloe and Marlley face personal challenges as the school year begins, and must leave boarding school and return home; Jaycee travels home to her Aboriginal mission in regional Victoria.

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole - Australia's Egyptian Mystery
programme.desc: Marc Fennell travels to the Pyramids of Giza, down the Nile and to the very heart of the British Empire to uncover the truth of how the ancient Egyptian mummified remains of a child ended up in Australia's oldest university.

programme.title: Dr Ann's Secret Lives - Bull Sharks
programme.desc: In northern Queensland, nature nerd Dr Ann Jones comes face to face with one of Australia's mightiest predators, the bull shark, and discovers there's much more to them than meets the eye.

programme.title: Dr Karl's How Things Work - Ice Cream
programme.desc: Dr Karl gets the scoop on how two of our most popular ice creams are made. Australians each devour around 18 litres of ice cream a year, making us the third largest population of ice cream eaters on the planet.

programme.title: ABC Late News Weekend - Episode 38
programme.desc: When you need to know what's happening turn to ABC News for coverage you can trust. Meredith Sheehan brings you the day's news by reporters in more locations across Australia, connecting communities and telling your stories.

programme.title: Offsiders - Episode 14
programme.desc: For 20 years, Offsiders has been here to unpack the week in sport. Abbey Gelmi and the panel discuss the latest AFL, NRL, Women's Rugby League State of Origin, Men's and Women's A-League Finals, Super Rugby and Super Netball.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero - Episode 6
programme.desc: Join acclaimed Torres Strait Islander chef and cultural ambassador Nornie Bero in Island Echoes, a vibrant journey through the breathtaking landscapes and rich traditions of her homeland.

programme.title: Field to Feast - Episode 2
programme.desc: You've heard of green asparagus but have you ever tried white asparagus. Thanh heads to a local farm to learn more about this fascinating variety. Topped off with a fine dining experience at a hip Melbourne restaurant.

programme.title: ABC News Overnight - Episode 38
programme.desc: The final word on the day's top stories, with the latest news and updated information from Australia's most trusted source of independent news. Presented by Meredith Sheehan.

programme.title: Common Cause - The Eye Doctor - Vietnam
programme.desc: In his final years, Australian eye surgeon Fred Hollows trained Vietnamese doctors in modern cataract surgery. Decades on, they and their students continue his mission, restoring sight to those who might otherwise go without.

programme.title: Compass - Surviving Gaza, Seeking Truth
programme.desc: Filmmaker Patrick Abboud meets frontline Palestinian journalists who risked everything to document the truth of Gaza. Now safe in Australia, they reveal extraordinary new evidence of the continued killing of journalists.

programme.title: Off Country - Episode 2
programme.desc: Xyz, Chloe and Marlley face personal challenges as the school year begins, and must leave boarding school and return home; Jaycee travels home to her Aboriginal mission in regional Victoria.

programme.title: I was actually there - Boxing Day Tsunami
programme.desc: A massive earthquake below the Indian Ocean unleashed the deadliest tsunami in history, killing 230,000 people. Those who were swept up in the wave had no idea of its scale, only of the devastation that unfolded before them.

programme.title: Play On - Episode 1
programme.desc: Connor O'Leary, a Cronulla surfer who now surfs for Japan. Pasifika rugby league players on their connection to their homeland. And Gronya Somerville, a badminton player exploring her ties to Chinese aristocracy.

programme.title: A Brief History of Cricket Down Under: The 80s - Gaining the Edge
programme.desc: New bat technology, the key to reverse-swing, slow bowling and team culture are all crucial elements in a quality team's march to victory. This episode reveals the ways teams playing in the '80s found that winning edge.

programme.title: Landline - Episode 13
programme.desc: Hosted by award-winning journalist Pip Courtney, Landline is Australia's only national agricultural television program covering stories from Australia's rural and regional heartland.

programme.title: Insiders - Episode 15
programme.desc: David Speers and the panel bring you all the reaction and fallout from the Farrer By-election, plus look ahead to Tuesday's Federal Budget, what we know so far and what the Treasurer's plan means for you and the economy.

programme.title: Offsiders - Episode 14
programme.desc: For 20 years, Offsiders has been here to unpack the week in sport. Abbey Gelmi and the panel discuss the latest AFL, NRL, Women's Rugby League State of Origin, Men's and Women's A-League Finals, Super Rugby and Super Netball.

programme.title: News Breakfast - Episode 93
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato bring you the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning, joined by Catherine Murphy with sport and Lillian Rangiah with weather.

programme.title: News Breakfast - Episode 93
programme.desc: As each day begins get the facts you need to stay informed. James Glenday and Emma Rebellato present the latest news, overnight developments and today's top stories, joined by Catherine Murphy, Nate Byrne and Lillian Rangiah.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings - Episode 93
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Gemma Veness.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon - Episode 93
programme.desc: The leading news at lunchtime. Kathryn Robinson brings you today's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: Man on Fire
programme.desc: In Mexico City, a former Marine swears vengeance on those who committed an unspeakable act against the family he was hired to protect.

programme.title: Two Mules for Sister Sara
programme.desc: When a former Civil War soldier saves a nun from a gang of bandits, they team up to help the Mexicans in their war against the French.

programme.title: Getting Even with Dad
programme.desc: When he is dropped off at the home of Ray Gleeson (Ted Danson), the father he hasn’t seen in years, Timmy (Macaulay Culkin) hopes that they’ll be able to spend some time together. But, unfortunately, Dad and his two klutzy cohorts are too busy with a get-rich-quick scheme, illegally paving the road to financial bliss...that is, until Timmy steps into their path. Recognizing that only he can set his father straight, this inventive kid puts an end to their plans and makes Ray do time...as a dad!

programme.title: Grade 2 Filipino
programme.desc: Matuto tungkol sa pangngalan, panghalip, salitang magkatugma, salitang naglalarawan, at pagbabaybay, pati na rin ang iba't ibang elemento ng kuwento at ang pag-uugnay ng sarili sa tekstong nabasa.

programme.title: Grade 2 Math
programme.desc: Study how to divide whole numbers up to 1000 including money and unit fractions. Learn more about straight and curved lines, flat and curved surfaces, and basic shapes and continuous patterns using two attributes.

programme.title: Grade 2 English
programme.desc: Discover what are word clines, synonyms, and antonyms. Identify major points and key themes in a story, and the difference between what is made-up and real in texts.

programme.title: Grade 2 Araling Panlipunan
programme.desc: Alamin ang kahalagahan ng mabuting paglilingkod ng mga namumuno sa pagsulong ng mga pangunahing hanapbuhay at pagtugon sa pangangailangan ng mga kasapi ng sariling komunidad.

programme.title: Grade 3 Filipino
programme.desc: Pag-aralan ang tambalang salita, pandiwa, pang-abay, at pang-ukol. Alamin kung paano magbigay ng sariling ideya, hinuha, pangunahin at sumusuportang kaisipan, tungkol sa kuwentong napakinggan.

programme.title: Grade 3 Math
programme.desc: Identify which fractions are proper and improper, similar and dissimilar and equivalent. Learn more about lines and symmetrical designs, continuous and repeating patterns, and mathematical sentences involving multiplication and division of whole numbers.

programme.title: Grade 3 English
programme.desc: Study what are homonyms, homographs, and hyponyms. Know more about the elements of a story, how to write it, and how to compare and contrast its information, alongside learning about words, phrases, and sentences with long vowels.

programme.title: Grade 3 Araling Panlipunan
programme.desc: Unawain at pahalagahan ang pagkakakilanlang kultural ng kinabibilangang rehiyon.

programme.title: Grade 3 Science
programme.desc: Discover how objects perform motion, and learn about the source of light, sound, heat and electricity, along with their uses.

programme.title: Grade 4 Filipino
programme.desc: Alamin kung paano wastong gamitin ang mga pang-abay, pang-uri, pang-angkop, pangatnig, salitang naglalarawan. Aralin kung paano magsulat ng pangungusap, talata, at balita, at kung paano magsuri ng mga kuwento at mga pahayag.

programme.title: Grade 4 Math
programme.desc: Learn about parallel and perpendicular lines, angles, triangles, quadrilaterals, continuous and repeating patterns and number sentences, and the concept of time, perimeter, area, and volume.

programme.title: Grade 4 English
programme.desc: Learn what are adverbs, signal words, and graphic organizers, and how to analyze a story and infer the speaker's tone, mood and purpose.

programme.title: Grade 4 Araling Panlipunan
programme.desc: Unawain ang bahaging ginagampanan ng pamahalaan sa lipunan, mga pinuno at iba pang naglilingkod sa pagkakaisa, kaayusan at kaunlaran ng bansa.

programme.title: Grade 4 Science
programme.desc: Know more about forces that change the shape, size or movement of objects and how light, heat and sound travel using various media.

programme.title: Grade 9 Filipino
programme.desc: Suriin ang iba't ibang uri ng panitikan tulad ng parabula, elehiya, at alamat, at ang pinagmulan ng mga salita at pag-uugnay ng sariling karanasan sa mga tekstong binasa.

programme.title: Grade 9 Math
programme.desc: Discover the key concepts of parallelograms and triangle similarity.

programme.title: Grade 9 English
programme.desc: Learn the difference of biases from prejudice and the relevance and validity of information presented.

programme.title: Grade 9 Araling Panlipunan
programme.desc: Pag-aralan ang mga pangunahing kaalaman tungkol sa pambansang ekonomiya bilang kabahagi sa pagpapabuti ng pamumuhay ng kapwa mamamayan tungo sa pambansang kaunlaran.

programme.title: Grade 9 Science
programme.desc: Know more about the volcanoes found in the Philippines, the factors that affect climate, the effects of changing climate, and how to adapt accordingly. Discover the relationship between the constellations in the sky and Earth's position along its orbit.
